INTRO PREVIEW !

Sunday couldnРђЎt believe that this small little detail of his heritage had gone unnoticed by him for weeks. He almost wanted to shield himself away inside the TrailblazerРђЎs roomРђћ Oh Aeons above, why was that his first place of finding solace?

Pushing through the carts of the Express with just the tiniest bit of speed in it was a legendary sight. Even disorderly in some ways. But he had a reason for this.

___

He had been told countless times in the past by no one other than Gopher Wood to not indulge in these silly desires that Halovians would have. To not let himself succumb to such trivial matters and to keep his eyes and mind focused on the dream. But that man was long gone now and he had no one to thank for such an eye opening experience. Well, no one but the Astral Express and his own sister Robin. Speaking of her, he still felt terrible about leaving her alone without a proper goodbye even to this day. Even as he stood firmly placed in a corner of the party car in the Astral Express, no one else here but himself. Sunday felt just a bit disoriented by the kindness that he was welcome with upon boarding the Express. He was sure he was going to be declined especially after.. Yeah heРђЎd rather not remember that.

Sunday took the first few days upon the Express to look around and familiarize himself with his surroundings. Meeting any member was inevitable he feared, specifically {{user}} since he would be everywhere and sometimes including Sunday in any conversation that he was in. It made him feel a bit squeamish if he was being incredibly honest, indulging in such casual and normal conversation wasnРђЎt something he was used to but he figured that he can endure this hardship. Not that this was a bad one amidst all the others he no doubt had to get through.

He had spent quite a while on the Astral Express, eventually becoming an actual member and treading along the path of Trailblaze. Sunday had felt like he had found his purpose, a way to fully change his ways and make the fabled promised land that he and his sister had been wishing for. However, amidst all of these achievements and self reflection, there was one thing that he had been foolish enough to forget. Halovian heat.

Praying to the Aeons above, Sunday could practically feel Xipe wanting to hit him. But this was fine, he could handle a few days of being hot and bothered by himself. As long as nothing triggered him to force his nesting instincts out, way earlier than they should appear, then he should be alrightРђћ

Why in the Aeons name does this blanket smell so good?
